design: 5 architecturally-distinct predictors (identity, mean, ridge, HistGBR, deep-ensemble) x 4-level pre-registered shift ladder; nonconformity = per-target RMS residual; split-conformal; 500 bootstrap; n_perturbations=4879 n_genes=2000

F1_severe_undercoverage.prediction: cross-donor coverage@0.90 < 0.88 for majority of predictors
F1_severe_undercoverage.ruling: FALSIFIED (FALSE). Coverage@0.90 is 0.88-0.91 across all 5 predictors under the pre-registered protocol — undercoverage is MILD, not severe. This OVERTURNS the earlier exploratory core-job headline (cov 0.69@0.90); that dramatic 'collapse to 0.52' figure was method-dependent (cross-CONDITION Rest->Stim task + different nonconformity split) and did NOT survive pre-registration. The frozen protocol caught our own overclaim — exactly the failure the red-team predicted.

F2_model_independence.ruling: SUPPORTED up to cross-donor (spread 0.03) — coverage is genuinely MODEL-INDEPENDENT across 5 unrelated architectures under mild-to-moderate shift. BUT model-independence BREAKS at cross-dataset shift (spread 0.437: identity over-covers to 1.0, deep-ensemble under-covers to 0.563). Honest nuance: calibration is model-agnostic under mild/moderate shift and becomes model-DEPENDENT only under the most severe (cross-cell-type/dataset) shift.

F3_ladder_monotonic.ruling: WEAK/PARTIAL. Gaps are ~0 (in-dist -0.016, held-out -0.02, cross-donor +0.005) then +0.056 at cross-dataset. Correct-signed severity trend but small in magnitude and not strongly monotonic in the mild regimes.

honest_headline: Under a pre-registered, well-powered protocol, split-conformal calibration of perturbation-effect prediction is REMARKABLY ROBUST and MODEL-INDEPENDENT (coverage@0.90 within 0.03 across 5 architectures) up to cross-donor shift; undercoverage is MILD (gap <=0.056) and grows, along with cross-model divergence, only under the most severe cross-dataset shift. The pre-registration FALSIFIED the project's own earlier 'severe undercoverage / abstain-don't-repair' headline, which was method-dependent.
redteam_caveats_incorporated: Earlier active-learning '67%->0% wasted budget' is an ORACLE handoff (deterministic 0.000±0.000), not a learned result — RETRACTED as a headline; will be reported only as an oracle upper bound if used at all.
Earlier E-Mondrian 'impossibility' reading is unsupported without an oracle-conditioning control (n_bins=3) — downgraded to 'did not help in this implementation', not a general claim.

'Gap magnitude IS the trust signal' is not prospectively computable without target-donor labels; a nested-LODO gap-TRANSPORT test is the required next step and is NOT yet done — claim held as a hypothesis, not a result.
merit_reading: This is a within-tier consolidation with an integrity WIN: the pre-registration caught a fragile prior headline before a reviewer could. The surviving positive claim (model-independent calibration up to cross-donor shift, breaking under severe shift) is clean and well-powered but is an EXPECTED-adjacent property (heavy novelty discount). Honest merit stays 78; the value here is credibility/robustness, not a tier jump.
